content.full_text:
Srr li
'fj
Tommi e L. SchLlette
925 seacoast Dr'
Imperi al Beach, Ca 92032
September 12, 1990
Fai r Political Practices Committee
428 "J" St., Suite 800
Sacramento, Ca 95814
Attn: Scott Hal labin
Re: Request for rul ing on possib'le conflict of interest
Oear Mr. Hal l abi n:
I am a member of the Citv Counc'i'l of lmperial Beach and a candidate
for Mayor in the November election.
I have received three different opinions from three different city
Atto.n"y" as to whether or not my current business location places
," in u- "conflict of interest" situation with regard to voting on
issues pertainihg to our Seacoast specific p'lan, which 'is a
beachfront development plan. f am requesting a ru'ling from you
so that I may end the confusion.
I am a licensed Real Estate Broker and am a partner in a Real
Estate firm, Bahja Realty, which does both real estate aales and
property management. We currently 'lease an office space at 925
Seacoast Dr., which is in the seacoast specific plan area.
The seacoast specific plan was adopted by the city councjl in 1984
and approved by the Coastal Commission in 1985. The origina'l
elements of the plan included a redevelopment agency and the use
of eminent domain. However, Lhe citizens of this community voted
down the redevelopment agency making the plan unworkab'le. Much
revision was ( and still 
is) necessary to come up with a viable
plan, requiring many independent issues to be considered and voted
upon by the city Counci l,
when I first came onto the cjty council in 1986, my office was not
located in the specific plan area, but was several blocks away.
When the opportunity arose to lease the new space on Seacoast Dr',
I asked Ron Null, City Attorney at that time, if such a move would
place me in confIjct of interest on any votes pertaining to the
seacoast specific plan, He said that there would be no conf 'l ict
as no change in my business would result from the move (i.e' sales
are generated city-wide and not iust within the plan area - in
fact, our office has been the agent for no sales in the plan area'
although v,/e do manage, for other owners, two properties in the plan
area ) .


Sometime later, as a result of a question by a local resident, Mr.
Richard Kuhlmeier, the Assistant City Attorney, Susan Reynolds,
told me she felt that there could be the "appearance of a confl ict
of interest" and that, for my own protection, I should "step down"
on all votes pertaining to the Seacoast specific plan.
I do not now, nor have I ever, felt myself to be in confl ict of
interest. My business is generated city-wide and not solely within
the area of the specific plan. Further, the opportunity for a real
estate off i ce to obtai n l i st ings on property for sal e and
management is not determined by it's locations, but rather by the
conticts and sol icitations of it's sales force. AII other realtors
operating in the Imperial Beach area have equal access to all
bus'i ness- generated in the plan area. No special benefi t would
accrue to me or my office by virtue of any vote I would make on the
speci f i c pl an.
The current City Attorney, Lynn McDougal , agrees with me' but
suggested that I bring the matter before you for clarification'
I would like to represent my constituents to the best of my abil'i ty
and would feel better able to do so if this issue were set to rest
once and for all. 
Therefore, I request a rul ing from you as to
whether or not my business location would constitute a confl ict of
interest on any votes pertaining to the S€acoast specific plan'
I would appreciate a ruling as soon as possible as this may end up
being a critical issue for my mayoral candidacy.
Sincerely,
cfu.-r,-,.'. *. )-/--*
Tommie L. Schuette
City Counci Iperson
City of Imperi al Beach
Home Address:
184 Elm Av.
Imperial beach, Ca. 92032
423-2232 wk
424- 7694 Res

sections.question: whether or not my business location would constitute a conflict of interest on any votes pertaining to the Seacoast specific plan
sections.question_synthetic: Does a city council member's real estate office location within a specific plan area create a conflict of interest when voting on issues pertaining to that plan, given that the business operates city-wide and has received no special benefits from the plan area?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a private party to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present. The legal question and reasoning are embedded in the letter narrative. Document quality is poor (OCR artifacts present) but content is largely legible.

embedding.summary: Tommie L. Schuette, an Imperial Beach City Council member and mayoral candidate, requests an FPPC ruling on whether his real estate firm's office location in the Seacoast specific plan area creates a conflict of interest when voting on plan-related matters. He argues his business operates city-wide with no special benefits from the plan area, but has received conflicting advice from multiple city attorneys.
